title: SOC 2 A1 — Disaster Recovery Test Execution and Evidence Timestamps

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOC 2 A1.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 A1.2 (Availability commitments) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.8.28: SOC 2 A1 — Disaster Recovery Test Execution and Evidence Timestamps.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.8.28' running on sourcetype dr:test_result and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=resilience` `sourcetype="dr:test_result"` (test_id, region_pair, rto_min, rpo_min, pass_fail, executed_by, _time); optional `snow:cmdb_ci` for scope
app: HTTP Event Collector, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
index=resilience sourcetype="dr:test_result" earliest=-400d
| bin _time span=90d as quarter
| stats latest(pass_fail) as outcome, latest(rto_min) as rto, latest(rpo_min) as rpo by quarter, region_pair
| eval ok=if(outcome="pass",1,0)
| eventstats sum(ok) as passes, count as quarters by region_pair
| where passes<4
| table region_pair, quarter, outcome, rto, rpo
description: A1 expects recovery commitments to be tested. Indexing DR runbook executions, RTO/RPO measurements, and failover test results with immutable timestamps proves tests occurred at the required frequency and outcomes were recorded.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes resilience and sourcetypes dr:test_result. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for A1 — Disaster Recovery Test Execution and Evidence Timestamps. SOC 2 is how we show we run our services responsibly, and we keep evidence that supports that story.
implementation: (1) Require one test per in-scope region_pair per quarter minimum—tune to policy; (2) integrate orchestration tool webhooks; (3) store raw logs in WORM; (4) failed `pass_fail` opens severity-2 problem; (5) map to customer SLA annex. Indexes required: `index=resilience`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=dr:test_result`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=resilience` `sourcetype="dr:test_result"` (test_id, region_pair, rto_min, rpo_min, pass_fail, executed_by, _time); optional `snow:cmdb_ci` for scope.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require one test per in-scope region_pair per quarter minimum—tune to policy; (2) integrate orchestration tool webhooks; (3) store raw logs in WORM; (4) failed `pass_fail` opens severity-2 problem; (5) map to customer SLA annex.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=resilience sourcetype="dr:test_result" earliest=-400d
| bin _time span=90d as quarter
| stats latest(pass_fail) as outcome, latest(rto_min) as rto, latest(rpo_min) as rpo by quarter, region_pair
| eval ok=if(outcome="pass",1,0)
| eventstats sum(ok) as passes, count as quarters by region_pair
| where passes<4
| table region_pair, quarter, outcome, rto, rpo
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 A1 — Disaster Recovery Test Execution and Evidence Timestamps** — A1 expects recovery commitments to be tested. Indexing DR runbook executions, RTO/RPO measurements, and failover test results with immutable timestamps proves tests occurred at the required frequency and outcomes were recorded.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=resilience` `sourcetype="dr:test_result"` (test_id, region_pair, rto_min, rpo_min, pass_fail, executed_by, _time); optional `snow:cmdb_ci` for scope. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: resilience; **sourcetype**: dr:test_result. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=resilience, sourcetype="dr:test_result", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **quarter, region_pair**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ok** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by region_pair** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where passes<4` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: ok, outcome, quarter, region_pair, rpo, rto.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (tests), Table (gaps), Single value (region_pairs behind).
knownFalsePositives: Control test failures during audit windows, immature control rollouts, or known compensating-control gaps.
